title: Ione Adair
origin: Parents came from Oregon and Indiana (1893); family lived in the McConnell mansion (1900-36)
occupation: County assessor; teacher; postal clerk; timber homesteader

title: Ernest Anderson
origin: Parents emigrated from Sweden and settled in the late nineties. Helen Anderson's husband
occupation: Farmer

title: Helen Kellberg Anderson
origin: Parents were Swedish settlers who came via Missouri (1906)
occupation: Farm wife

title: Walter Benscoter
origin: Parents were homesteaders from Michigan (1885)
occupation: Farmer

title: Arthur Bjerke
origin: Came from Norway with family, who homesteaded in 1891
occupation: Farmer; carpenter; logger

title: Louis Boas
origin: Came from Boise to attend the university
occupation: Editor of Moscow Daily Star-Mirror and Daily Idahonian (1926-1966)

title: Mary McConnell Borah
origin: William J. McConnell, her father, first came in 1879 from the Boise Basin and was elected Idaho governor (1892); she came with her mother from Oregon (1888)
occupation: Wife of Idaho Senator William Borah

title: Carol Ryrie Brink
origin: Grandfather was a Moscow doctor (1887) trained in Missouri; Father came from Scotland (1889)
occupation: Novelist; homemaker. Caddie Woodlawn won the Newberry Award; Buffalo Coat; Snow in the River and Strangers in the Forest are filled with detail about early local life

title: Frank Brocke
origin: Parents' families came from Germany; father was born at Genesee, mother in Kansas
occupation: President of First Bank of Troy; where he worked for forty-seven years; chairman of school board

title: Edna Johnson Butterfield
origin: Family came from Michigan (1888)
occupation: Farm wife

title: Fannie Cuthbert Byers
origin: Parents were born in Scotland, came from Kansas and homesteaded (1888)
occupation: Farm wife; pea processor; harvest cook

title: Lola Gamble Clyde
origin: Father emigrated from Ireland and became the area's first Presbyterian minister (1880); mother was from Victoria, British Columbia
occupation: Teacher; farm wife; local historian; frequently addresses groups and aids researchers

title: Ada Hill Crow
origin: Family moved from Junction City, Oregon and homesteaded (1887)
occupation: Farm wife

title: John Cushman
origin: n/a
occupation: n/a

title: John Diamantis
origin: Emigrated from Klitsos, Greece in 1909
occupation: Sawmiller; logger

title: Jennie Halverson Driscoll
origin: Parents homesteaded after emigrating from Norway via Astoria (early 1880's)
occupation: Farm wife

title: Dora Otter Fleener
origin: Family moved from South Dakota in 1902
occupation: Farm wife; housekeeper; author of Coming West from South Dakota

title: Elmer Flodin
origin: Parents were Swedish homesteaders (early 1880's)
occupation: Farmer; logger

title: Abe MacGregor Goff
origin: Moved to Moscow to attend university; father homesteaded near Rosalia,Wash
occupation: Lawyer; state legislator; U.S. Congressman; chairman of Interstate Commerce Commission

title: Alice Henry Jackson
origin: Mother was Nez Perce; father moved from Asotin County, Washington (c.1890)
occupation: Farm wife

title: Charles Jelleberg
origin: Parents were Norwegian homesteaders (c. 1890)
occupation: Horse teamster; sawmiller

title: Clarence Johnson
origin: Parents were homesteaders from Sweden (1884)
occupation: Farmer

title: Walter Johnson
origin: Parents were Swedish immigrants who moved from Minnesota (1882)
occupation: Accountant

title: Agnes Healy Jones
origin: Mother's parents (Tierneys) were the first white settlers of the Genesee area,coming from Kansas in 1870; father emigrated from Ireland (early 1870s)
occupation: Farm wife; waitress

title: William Kauder
origin: Homesteaded alongside parents, after moving from Illinois (1889)
occupation: Farmer

title: Mi & Marie Lee Lew
origin: Came to Spokane from China in 1920
occupation: Restaurateur

title: John B. Miller
origin: Parents came from Minnesota (1902)
occupation: Geologist; author of The Trees Grew Tall (1972); Bovill area history

title: William Morgan
origin: Parents farmed near Nezperce after leaving Kansas (1898)
occupation: Owner of Morgan Brothers; food and equipment distributors

title: Elsie Nelson
origin: Parents were Swedish homesteaders (1886)
occupation: Head cook at Hotel Moscow; teacher; author of Today Is Ours

title: Carl Olson
origin: Parents emigrated from Sweden and homesteaded (1889)
occupation: Thresherman; operator of gas station and car dealership; sawmiller; miner; Troy councilman for 24 years

title: Oscar & Hazel Olson
origin: Moved from central Idaho in the early forties
occupation: Homemaker; teacher; camp flunkey

title: Lillian Woodworth Otness
origin: Grandfather was an original founder of Moscow, moving from eastern Oregon (1871); father came from Montana (1877)
occupation: Teacher of English and physical education in college and high school; homemaker;

title: Clarice Moody Sampson
origin: Parents came from Utah (1892)
occupation: Homemaker; teacher; clerk at David's Store

title: Harry Sampson
origin: Family came from Wisconsin in 1902
occupation: Manager of men's clothing department at David's Department Store for nearly forty years

title: John Sanderson
origin: Byers Sanderson's brother
occupation: Maintenance man for Potlatch Lumber Company; photographer

title: Eugene Settle
origin: His was one of the few black families to settle in the area; parents grew up in Mississippi, came in 1899
occupation: Warehouse superintendent for Latah County Grain Growers ; farmer

title: Emma Christenson Shirrod
origin: Parents were Norwegian homesteaders in the 1870's
